summary: The suite covers all major requirements: reading version.txt and package.json, all three bump types (patch/minor/major), version file updates, changelog generation, integration orchestration, and workflow structure. Every public function has at least one test. Rigor is the weak spot: missing tests for package.json lacking a 'version' field, feat/fix with scopes in Get-CommitBumpType, major dominating when both breaking and feat commits appear, BREAKING CHANGE footer in New-ChangelogEntry, malformed JSON in Update-VersionFile, and an existing CHANGELOG.md without a header line in Update-Changelog. Temp file cleanup is done inline rather than in AfterEach blocks, so failures leave artifacts and create false coupling. Using New-TemporaryFile (no extension) for the package.json test works only because the implementation falls back to content detection—slightly brittle. The actionlint test correctly skips when the tool is absent. The act-based harness (Run-ActTests.ps1) is separate from Pester and would not be discovered by Invoke-Pester, meaning the end-to-end pipeline assertions aren't part of the formal test run. Despite these gaps, the core logic tests are readable, well-named, and would catch most regressions in the happy paths.
